paper_title: Eocene Baltic Amber Fossil Mite Study

methods.description: The process of preparing the amber fragment containing the fossil mite for study, including trimming, embedding, and polishing to provide clear views of the specimen.

steps.description: The fragment measuring 4.5x3.5x0.8 mm, containing the mite, was trimmed from the original piece embedded in polyester resin and then polished on two sides.
steps.searchable_summary: Trim and polish amber fragment containing fossil mite

steps.description: Following protocols of Sidorchuk and Vorontsov (2018), to provide dorsal and ventral aspects of the mite.
steps.searchable_summary: Follow specific protocols to expose dorsal and ventral aspects

steps.description: For collection storage, after the study the specimen was covered by the same artificial resin.
steps.searchable_summary: Cover specimen with artificial resin for storage

methods.description: The method of capturing images of the fossil mite using a compound microscope and digital camera, followed by focus stacking to enhance image clarity.

steps.description: For imaging, a Nikon E-800 compound microscope with dry (10x) and water (40x and 60x) objectives was used.
steps.searchable_summary: Use compound microscope with specific objectives for imaging

steps.description: As the body of the mite was mostly non-translucent, most of the micrographs were made in reflected light that limited the resolution.
steps.searchable_summary: Capture micrographs in reflected light due to non-translucent body

steps.description: Stacks of images, comprising multiple focal planes, were obtained with an Olympus OM-D E-M10-II digital camera.
steps.searchable_summary: Capture image stacks with digital camera across multiple focal planes

steps.description: Final images were focus-stacked using Helicon Focus Pro 7.6.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform focus stacking on images using Helicon Focus Pro

steps.description: Image stacks are available through Figshare.

methods.description: The approach to using specific terminology, classification systems, and measurement techniques for analyzing the morphological structures of the fossil mite.

steps.description: Terminology used for morphological structures follows those of Evans (1992) and Alberti and Coons (1999).
steps.searchable_summary: Apply specific terminology for morphological structures

steps.description: Classification and systematic concepts generally follow Lindquist et al. (2009) and Beaulieu et al. (2011).
steps.searchable_summary: Use classification and systematic concepts from specific sources

steps.description: Measurements of structures are given in micrometers (µm) and rounded to the nearest 5 if beyond 30 mm.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure structures in micrometers and round appropriately

steps.description: Length, width and height relates in each case to the maximal dimension visible.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine maximal dimensions for length, width, and height

steps.description: As orientations of structures in amber were suboptimal, the measurements are to be treated as minimal estimates.
steps.searchable_summary: Consider measurements as minimal estimates due to suboptimal orientations
